serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of this condiment is approximately 15 g (one small dipping bowl), as used at Din Tai Fung alongside dumplings and noodles.

nutrient_source.title: Chili sauce, ready-to-serve (USDA SR Legacy NDB 06164); adapted with black vinegar component reference from USDA FoodData Central black vinegar/rice vinegar entries
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy and Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for chilli black vinegar sauce exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy 'Chili sauce, ready-to-serve' (NDB 06164) as the primary macro anchor, with sodium and sugar cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central entry for chili sauce (FDC ID 172231). Black vinegar contribution (Zhenjiang/Chinkiang vinegar) is referenced from USDA FoodData Central rice vinegar entry (FDC ID 173469) for acidity and minor carbohydrate adjustment. Energy recalculated using Atwater factors. This is an adapted estimate; exact restaurant formulation is proprietary.

gi_source.title: Vinegar and pungent sauces — GI analogous estimate based on Sugiyama et al. (2003) 'Glycemic index of single and mixed meal foods among common Japanese foods with white rice as a reference food' and Foster-Powell et al. (2002) International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values
gi_source.organization: European Journal of Clinical Nutrition / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chilli black vinegar sauce. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on: (1) vinegar-based condiments are well-documented to lower postprandial glycemic response (Sugiyama et al. 2003, EJCN); (2) chilli sauce analogues in Foster-Powell et al. (2002) international GI table report GI values in the 30–45 range for vinegar-containing sauces; (3) the high acidity from black vinegar (acetic acid) is established to attenuate starch digestion. GL per 15 g serving = (35 × 15.8 × 15/100) / 100 = 0.83, rounded to 0.8. This is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (820 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should strictly limit this sauce to no more than one small dipping portion (≈15 g, contributing approximately 123 mg sodium) per meal, as regular or liberal use can significantly contribute to daily sodium excess beyond the WHO recommended 2000 mg/day limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (estimated GI 35) due to acetic acid from black vinegar attenuating postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: The acetic acid content of black vinegar in this sauce is associated with reduced postprandial blood glucose elevation; however, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should still monitor portion size due to the moderate sugar content (10.2 g per 100 g) and use no more than one small dipping portion per meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium content raises cardiovascular risk; fat content is low and cholesterol is negligible
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While the fat and cholesterol profile of this sauce is favourable for cardiovascular health (fat 0.4 g/100 g, cholesterol 0 mg/100 g), the high sodium content (820 mg/100 g) poses a meaningful cardiovascular risk if consumed in excess, and individuals with cardiovascular disease should restrict use to small condiment portions only.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Signature Chilli Black Vinegar Sauce (特色香辣黑醋酱) is a Chinese condiment sauce combining red chilli, Zhenjiang (Chinkiang) black vinegar, garlic, sugar, and salt. It is a signature dipping sauce served at Din Tai Fung restaurants in Singapore and internationally, most commonly paired with xiao long bao (soup dumplings), steamed dumplings, and noodle dishes. The sauce has a complex flavour profile: tangy and acidic from the aged black vinegar, moderately spicy from the chilli, with a slight sweetness and savoury depth from garlic and salt. The texture is smooth and pourable. Black vinegar (镇江香醋, Zhenjiang xiāng cù) is a traditional Chinese fermented rice vinegar from Zhejiang province, prized for its mellow acidity and umami undertones. Nutritionally, this sauce is low in fat (0.4 g/100 g) and moderate in carbohydrates (15.8 g/100 g), with a significant sodium content of 820 mg/100 g, making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The acetic acid from black vinegar has been associated in peer-reviewed literature with attenuation of postprandial blood glucose, giving this sauce a low estimated glycemic index of approximately 35. A typical serving is 15 g (one small dipping bowl), contributing approximately 11 kcal and 123 mg sodium. The sauce is vegetarian, vegan, and halal-suitable. It is not gluten-free as black vinegar may contain wheat-derived components.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this proprietary restaurant sauce exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy chili sauce (NDB 06164) with black vinegar component cross-referencing. GI is an analogous estimate based on peer-reviewed vinegar-condiment literature. Confidence is medium rather than low because the adaptation is methodologically sound and the source anchor is a well-validated USDA entry.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates based on USDA SR Legacy chili sauce (NDB 06164) and USDA FoodData Central rice vinegar (FDC ID 173469); exact Din Tai Fung formulation is proprietary and not publicly disclosed. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a plant-based vinegar-chilli condiment with no hydrogenated fats. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as all ingredients are plant-derived. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant-based condiment with no animal-derived ingredients. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this condiment contains no vitamin D sources. 6. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source entry for chili sauce. 7. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source entry. 8. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source entry. 9. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source entry. 10. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source entry. 11. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source entry. 12. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source entry. 13. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source entry. 14. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source entry. 15. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source entry. 16. GI is set to 35 (low) as an analogous estimate; direct GI measurement of this specific product is recommended for higher confidence. 17. Gluten-free flag is false as traditional Zhenjiang black vinegar may contain wheat; individuals with coeliac disease should verify with the restaurant. 18. Price data (SGD 4.91) preserved from field survey source record for reference only; not used as a nutritional evidence source.
